---
title: "Framebuffers & Post-Processing"
subtitle: "Computer Graphics - Screen Effects and Rendering to Textures"
author: "Gustavo Reis"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: dracula
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    preview-links: auto
    logo: "images/OpenGL/Opengl-logo.svg"
    css: custom.css
    footer: "Framebuffers and Post-Processing"
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    highlight-style: github-dark
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
---

## Introduction

So far, we've been rendering directly to the **default framebuffer** (the screen).

::: {.fragment}
**What if** we could render to a texture instead?
:::

::: {.fragment}
This opens up powerful possibilities:

- Post-processing effects (blur, HDR, bloom)
- Mirrors and reflections
- Shadow mapping (we've seen this!)
- Render to texture for UI
- Screen-space effects
:::

---

# Understanding Framebuffers

## What is a Framebuffer?

::: {.callout-important}
A **framebuffer** is a collection of buffers that can be used as the destination for rendering.
:::

::: {.incremental}
- **Color attachments**: Store color data (can have multiple)
- **Depth attachment**: Store depth information
- **Stencil attachment**: Store stencil values
- Can attach **textures** or **renderbuffers**
:::

## Default vs Custom Framebuffers

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Default Framebuffer:**

- Created by windowing system
- Displays on screen
- Has color, depth, stencil buffers
- One per window/context
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Custom Framebuffer:**

- Created by you
- Can render to textures
- Configurable attachments
- Off-screen rendering
- Multiple framebuffers possible
:::
:::

## Texture vs Renderbuffer

| Feature | Texture | Renderbuffer |
|---------|---------|--------------|
| **Can sample in shader** | ✓ Yes | ✗ No |
| **Can write to** | ✓ Yes | ✓ Yes |
| **Performance** | Slower | Faster |
| **Use case** | Need to read back | Don't need to read |

::: {.callout-tip}
Use **texture** for post-processing (need to sample). Use **renderbuffer** for depth/stencil when not sampling.
:::

---

# Creating Framebuffers

## Basic Framebuffer Setup

```cpp
// 1. Generate framebuffer
unsigned int fbo;
glGenFramebuffers(1, &fbo);
glBindFramebuffer(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, fbo);

// 2. Create color texture attachment
unsigned int textureColorbuffer;
glGenTextures(1, &textureColorbuffer);
glBindTexture(GL_TEXTURE_2D, textureColorbuffer);
glTexImage2D(GL_TEXTURE_2D, 0, GL_RGB, SCR_WIDTH, SCR_HEIGHT, 0,
             GL_RGB, GL_UNSIGNED_BYTE, NULL);
glTexParameteri(GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_MIN_FILTER, GL_LINEAR);
glTexParameteri(GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_MAG_FILTER, GL_LINEAR);

// Attach texture to framebuffer
glFramebufferTexture2D(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, GL_COLOR_ATTACHMENT0,
                      GL_TEXTURE_2D, textureColorbuffer, 0);
```

## Adding Depth and Stencil

```cpp
// 3. Create renderbuffer for depth and stencil
unsigned int rbo;
glGenRenderbuffers(1, &rbo);
glBindRenderbuffer(GL_RENDERBUFFER, rbo);
glRenderbufferStorage(GL_RENDERBUFFER, GL_DEPTH24_STENCIL8,
                     SCR_WIDTH, SCR_HEIGHT);

// Attach renderbuffer to framebuffer
glFramebufferRenderbuffer(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, GL_DEPTH_STENCIL_ATTACHMENT,
                         GL_RENDERBUFFER, rbo);

// 4. Check if framebuffer is complete
if (glCheckFramebufferStatus(GL_FRAMEBUFFER) != GL_FRAMEBUFFER_COMPLETE)
    std::cerr << "ERROR::FRAMEBUFFER:: Framebuffer is not complete!" << '\n';

// Unbind framebuffer
glBindFramebuffer(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, 0);
```

## Framebuffer Class {.smaller}

```cpp
class Framebuffer {
public:
    Framebuffer(int width, int height) : width_(width), height_(height) {
        glGenFramebuffers(1, &fbo_);
        glBindFramebuffer(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, fbo_);
        
        // Color texture
        glGenTextures(1, &colorTexture_);
        glBindTexture(GL_TEXTURE_2D, colorTexture_);
        glTexImage2D(GL_TEXTURE_2D, 0, GL_RGB, width, height, 0,
                     GL_RGB, GL_UNSIGNED_BYTE, nullptr);
        glTexParameteri(GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_MIN_FILTER, GL_LINEAR);
        glTexParameteri(GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_MAG_FILTER, GL_LINEAR);
        glFramebufferTexture2D(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, GL_COLOR_ATTACHMENT0,
                              GL_TEXTURE_2D, colorTexture_, 0);
        
        // Depth + stencil renderbuffer
        glGenRenderbuffers(1, &rbo_);
        glBindRenderbuffer(GL_RENDERBUFFER, rbo_);
        glRenderbufferStorage(GL_RENDERBUFFER, GL_DEPTH24_STENCIL8,
                             width, height);
        glFramebufferRenderbuffer(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, GL_DEPTH_STENCIL_ATTACHMENT,
                                 GL_RENDERBUFFER, rbo_);
        
        if (glCheckFramebufferStatus(GL_FRAMEBUFFER) != GL_FRAMEBUFFER_COMPLETE)
            throw std::runtime_error("Framebuffer is not complete!");
        
        glBindFramebuffer(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, 0);
    }
    
    ~Framebuffer() {
        glDeleteFramebuffers(1, &fbo_);
        glDeleteTextures(1, &colorTexture_);
        glDeleteRenderbuffers(1, &rbo_);
    }
    
    void bind() const {
        glBindFramebuffer(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, fbo_);
        glViewport(0, 0, width_, height_);
    }
    
    void unbind() const {
        glBindFramebuffer(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, 0);
    }
    
    unsigned int getColorTexture() const { return colorTexture_; }
    
private:
    unsigned int fbo_;
    unsigned int colorTexture_;
    unsigned int rbo_;
    int width_, height_;
};
```

---

# Rendering to Framebuffer

## Two-Pass Rendering

```cpp
// First pass: Render scene to framebuffer
framebuffer.bind();
glClearColor(0.1f, 0.1f, 0.1f, 1.0f);
glClear(GL_COLOR_BUFFER_BIT | GL_DEPTH_BUFFER_BIT);
glEnable(GL_DEPTH_TEST);

// Draw your scene normally
renderScene();

// Second pass: Render framebuffer texture to screen quad
framebuffer.unbind();
glClearColor(1.0f, 1.0f, 1.0f, 1.0f);
glClear(GL_COLOR_BUFFER_BIT);
glDisable(GL_DEPTH_TEST);

screenShader.use();
glBindVertexArray(quadVAO);
glBindTexture(GL_TEXTURE_2D, framebuffer.getColorTexture());
glDrawArrays(GL_TRIANGLES, 0, 6);
```

## Screen Quad Setup

```cpp
// Screen quad vertices (NDC, covering entire screen)
float quadVertices[] = {
    // positions   // texCoords
    -1.0f,  1.0f,  0.0f, 1.0f,
    -1.0f, -1.0f,  0.0f, 0.0f,
     1.0f, -1.0f,  1.0f, 0.0f,

    -1.0f,  1.0f,  0.0f, 1.0f,
     1.0f, -1.0f,  1.0f, 0.0f,
     1.0f,  1.0f,  1.0f, 1.0f
};

unsigned int quadVAO, quadVBO;
glGenVertexArrays(1, &quadVAO);
glGenBuffers(1, &quadVBO);

glBindVertexArray(quadVAO);
glBindBuffer(GL_ARRAY_BUFFER, quadVBO);
glBufferData(GL_ARRAY_BUFFER, sizeof(quadVertices), quadVertices, GL_STATIC_DRAW);

glEnableVertexAttribArray(0);
glVertexAttribPointer(0, 2, GL_FLOAT, GL_FALSE, 4 * sizeof(float), (void*)0);
glEnableVertexAttribArray(1);
glVertexAttribPointer(1, 2, GL_FLOAT, GL_FALSE, 4 * sizeof(float),
                     (void*)(2 * sizeof(float)));
```

## Basic Screen Shader

**Vertex Shader:**
```glsl
#version 330 core
layout (location = 0) in vec2 aPos;
layout (location = 1) in vec2 aTexCoords;

out vec2 TexCoords;

void main() {
    gl_Position = vec4(aPos.x, aPos.y, 0.0, 1.0);
    TexCoords = aTexCoords;
}
```

**Fragment Shader:**
```glsl
#version 330 core
out vec4 FragColor;

in vec2 TexCoords;

uniform sampler2D screenTexture;

void main() {
    FragColor = texture(screenTexture, TexCoords);
}
```

---

# Post-Processing Effects

## Inversion Effect

```glsl
void main() {
    vec3 color = texture(screenTexture, TexCoords).rgb;
    FragColor = vec4(1.0 - color, 1.0);  // Invert colors
}
```

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Original:**
![](https://learnopengl.com/img/advanced/framebuffers_screen_texture.png)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Inverted:**
![](https://learnopengl.com/img/advanced/framebuffers_inverse.png)
:::
:::

## Grayscale Effect

```glsl
void main() {
    vec3 color = texture(screenTexture, TexCoords).rgb;
    
    // Average method (simple but not perceptually accurate)
    // float gray = (color.r + color.g + color.b) / 3.0;
    
    // Weighted method (better - matches human perception)
    float gray = 0.2126 * color.r + 0.7152 * color.g + 0.0722 * color.b;
    
    FragColor = vec4(vec3(gray), 1.0);
}
```

![](https://learnopengl.com/img/advanced/framebuffers_grayscale.png){width=60%}

## Kernel/Convolution Effects

::: {.callout-tip}
**Convolution**: Sample surrounding pixels with a kernel matrix to create effects!
:::

```glsl
const float offset = 1.0 / 300.0;  // Offset for neighboring pixels

void main() {
    vec2 offsets[9] = vec2[](
        vec2(-offset,  offset), vec2( 0.0,    offset), vec2( offset,  offset),
        vec2(-offset,  0.0),    vec2( 0.0,    0.0),    vec2( offset,  0.0),
        vec2(-offset, -offset), vec2( 0.0,   -offset), vec2( offset, -offset)
    );

    // Sharpen kernel
    float kernel[9] = float[](
        -1, -1, -1,
        -1,  9, -1,
        -1, -1, -1
    );
    
    vec3 sampleTex[9];
    for(int i = 0; i < 9; i++)
        sampleTex[i] = texture(screenTexture, TexCoords + offsets[i]).rgb;
    
    vec3 col = vec3(0.0);
    for(int i = 0; i < 9; i++)
        col += sampleTex[i] * kernel[i];
    
    FragColor = vec4(col, 1.0);
}
```

## Common Kernels

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Sharpen:**
```
-1 -1 -1
-1  9 -1
-1 -1 -1
```

**Blur:**
```
1  2  1
2  4  2
1  2  1
(divide by 16)
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Edge Detection:**
```
 1  1  1
 1 -8  1
 1  1  1
```

**Emboss:**
```
-2 -1  0
-1  1  1
 0  1  2
```
:::
:::

## Blur Effect {.smaller}

```glsl
#version 330 core
out vec4 FragColor;

in vec2 TexCoords;

uniform sampler2D screenTexture;

void main() {
    const float offset = 1.0 / 300.0;
    vec2 offsets[9] = vec2[](
        vec2(-offset,  offset), vec2( 0.0,    offset), vec2( offset,  offset),
        vec2(-offset,  0.0),    vec2( 0.0,    0.0),    vec2( offset,  0.0),
        vec2(-offset, -offset), vec2( 0.0,   -offset), vec2( offset, -offset)
    );

    float kernel[9] = float[](
        1.0 / 16, 2.0 / 16, 1.0 / 16,
        2.0 / 16, 4.0 / 16, 2.0 / 16,
        1.0 / 16, 2.0 / 16, 1.0 / 16
    );
    
    vec3 sampleTex[9];
    for(int i = 0; i < 9; i++)
        sampleTex[i] = texture(screenTexture, TexCoords + offsets[i]).rgb;
        
    vec3 col = vec3(0.0);
    for(int i = 0; i < 9; i++)
        col += sampleTex[i] * kernel[i];
    
    FragColor = vec4(col, 1.0);
}
```

![](https://learnopengl.com/img/advanced/framebuffers_blur.png){width=50%}

## Edge Detection

```glsl
float kernel[9] = float[](
    1,  1, 1,
    1, -8, 1,
    1,  1, 1
);

// Apply kernel (same code as before)
vec3 col = vec3(0.0);
for(int i = 0; i < 9; i++)
    col += sampleTex[i] * kernel[i];

FragColor = vec4(col, 1.0);
```

![](https://learnopengl.com/img/advanced/framebuffers_edge_detection.png){width=50%}

---

# Advanced Effects

## Gaussian Blur (High Quality)

::: {.callout-tip}
**Gaussian blur** is more realistic than box blur. Use **separable** approach for performance!
:::

**Two-pass approach:**

1. **Horizontal blur pass**: Blur horizontally
2. **Vertical blur pass**: Blur vertically

**Why?** 2D Gaussian is separable:

- 9×9 kernel: 81 samples
- Two 1×9 kernels: 9 + 9 = 18 samples!

## Gaussian Blur Implementation {.smaller}

**Horizontal Pass:**
```glsl
#version 330 core
out vec4 FragColor;

in vec2 TexCoords;

uniform sampler2D image;
uniform float weight[5] = float[] (0.227027, 0.1945946, 0.1216216, 
                                   0.054054, 0.016216);

void main() {
    vec2 tex_offset = 1.0 / textureSize(image, 0); // Size of single texel
    vec3 result = texture(image, TexCoords).rgb * weight[0];
    
    for(int i = 1; i < 5; ++i) {
        result += texture(image, TexCoords + vec2(tex_offset.x * i, 0.0)).rgb * weight[i];
        result += texture(image, TexCoords - vec2(tex_offset.x * i, 0.0)).rgb * weight[i];
    }
    FragColor = vec4(result, 1.0);
}
```

**Vertical pass: Same but with `vec2(0.0, tex_offset.y * i)`**

## Bloom Effect

::: {.callout-important}
**Bloom**: Bright areas "glow" by blurring bright regions and adding them back to the original image.
:::

**Steps:**

1. Extract bright areas (threshold)
2. Blur bright areas (Gaussian blur)
3. Combine with original image

## Bloom Implementation {.smaller}

**Extract Bright Areas:**
```glsl
#version 330 core
layout (location = 0) out vec4 FragColor;
layout (location = 1) out vec4 BrightColor;

in vec2 TexCoords;

uniform sampler2D image;

void main() {
    vec3 color = texture(image, TexCoords).rgb;
    FragColor = vec4(color, 1.0);
    
    // Check if fragment is bright enough
    float brightness = dot(color, vec3(0.2126, 0.7152, 0.0722));
    if(brightness > 1.0)
        BrightColor = vec4(color, 1.0);
    else
        BrightColor = vec4(0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0);
}
```

**Combine:**
```glsl
uniform sampler2D scene;
uniform sampler2D bloomBlur;

void main() {
    vec3 hdrColor = texture(scene, TexCoords).rgb;
    vec3 bloomColor = texture(bloomBlur, TexCoords).rgb;
    FragColor = vec4(hdrColor + bloomColor, 1.0);
}
```

## HDR and Tone Mapping

::: {.callout-important}
**HDR**: High Dynamic Range allows values beyond [0, 1], then **tone map** back to display range.
:::

```glsl
// HDR framebuffer uses GL_RGB16F or GL_RGB32F
glTexImage2D(GL_TEXTURE_2D, 0, GL_RGB16F, width, height, 0,
             GL_RGB, GL_FLOAT, NULL);

// In fragment shader:
vec3 hdrColor = texture(hdrBuffer, TexCoords).rgb;

// Reinhard tone mapping
vec3 mapped = hdrColor / (hdrColor + vec3(1.0));

// Exposure tone mapping
float exposure = 1.0;
vec3 mapped = vec3(1.0) - exp(-hdrColor * exposure);

// Gamma correction
mapped = pow(mapped, vec3(1.0 / 2.2));

FragColor = vec4(mapped, 1.0);
```

## Chromatic Aberration

```glsl
// Simulate lens distortion - separate RGB channels
vec2 dir = TexCoords - vec2(0.5);  // Direction from center
float dist = length(dir);
dir = normalize(dir);

// Offset each channel differently
vec2 offset = dir * dist * 0.01;

float r = texture(screenTexture, TexCoords + offset).r;
float g = texture(screenTexture, TexCoords).g;
float b = texture(screenTexture, TexCoords - offset).b;

FragColor = vec4(r, g, b, 1.0);
```

## Vignette Effect

```glsl
// Darken edges of screen
vec2 uv = TexCoords;
vec3 color = texture(screenTexture, uv).rgb;

// Distance from center
float dist = length(uv - vec2(0.5));

// Vignette strength
float vignette = smoothstep(0.8, 0.2, dist);

FragColor = vec4(color * vignette, 1.0);
```

---

# Multiple Render Targets (MRT)

## What is MRT?

::: {.callout-tip}
**MRT**: Render to multiple textures simultaneously from a single pass!
:::

**Use cases:**

- Deferred rendering (G-buffer)
- Bloom (separate bright areas)
- Motion blur (velocity buffer)
- Any multi-output effect

## MRT Setup {.smaller}

```cpp
// Create framebuffer with multiple color attachments
unsigned int gBuffer;
glGenFramebuffers(1, &gBuffer);
glBindFramebuffer(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, gBuffer);

// Position texture
unsigned int gPosition;
glGenTextures(1, &gPosition);
glBindTexture(GL_TEXTURE_2D, gPosition);
glTexImage2D(GL_TEXTURE_2D, 0, GL_RGB16F, SCR_WIDTH, SCR_HEIGHT, 0,
             GL_RGB, GL_FLOAT, NULL);
glFramebufferTexture2D(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, GL_COLOR_ATTACHMENT0,
                      GL_TEXTURE_2D, gPosition, 0);

// Normal texture
unsigned int gNormal;
glGenTextures(1, &gNormal);
glBindTexture(GL_TEXTURE_2D, gNormal);
glTexImage2D(GL_TEXTURE_2D, 0, GL_RGB16F, SCR_WIDTH, SCR_HEIGHT, 0,
             GL_RGB, GL_FLOAT, NULL);
glFramebufferTexture2D(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, GL_COLOR_ATTACHMENT1,
                      GL_TEXTURE_2D, gNormal, 0);

// Color + Specular texture
unsigned int gAlbedoSpec;
glGenTextures(1, &gAlbedoSpec);
glBindTexture(GL_TEXTURE_2D, gAlbedoSpec);
glTexImage2D(GL_TEXTURE_2D, 0, GL_RGBA, SCR_WIDTH, SCR_HEIGHT, 0,
             GL_RGBA, GL_UNSIGNED_BYTE, NULL);
glFramebufferTexture2D(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, GL_COLOR_ATTACHMENT2,
                      GL_TEXTURE_2D, gAlbedoSpec, 0);

// Tell OpenGL which color attachments we'll use
unsigned int attachments[3] = {
    GL_COLOR_ATTACHMENT0, GL_COLOR_ATTACHMENT1, GL_COLOR_ATTACHMENT2
};
glDrawBuffers(3, attachments);
```

## MRT Shader

```glsl
#version 330 core
layout (location = 0) out vec3 gPosition;
layout (location = 1) out vec3 gNormal;
layout (location = 2) out vec4 gAlbedoSpec;

in vec3 FragPos;
in vec3 Normal;
in vec2 TexCoords;

uniform sampler2D texture_diffuse1;
uniform sampler2D texture_specular1;

void main() {
    // Output to multiple textures
    gPosition = FragPos;
    gNormal = normalize(Normal);
    gAlbedoSpec.rgb = texture(texture_diffuse1, TexCoords).rgb;
    gAlbedoSpec.a = texture(texture_specular1, TexCoords).r;
}
```

---

# Performance Considerations

## Optimization Tips

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Memory:**

- Use appropriate texture formats
- Consider resolution (don't need full res for all effects)
- Reuse framebuffers when possible
- Clean up unused framebuffers
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Rendering:**

- Minimize FBO switches
- Batch post-processing passes
- Use lower precision when acceptable
- Profile your effects
:::
:::

## Resolution Considerations

```cpp
// Don't always need full resolution!

// Full resolution for main scene
Framebuffer mainFBO(width, height);

// Half resolution for blur (cheaper, often imperceptible)
Framebuffer blurFBO(width / 2, height / 2);

// Quarter resolution for bloom
Framebuffer bloomFBO(width / 4, height / 4);
```

::: {.callout-tip}
Blur and bloom work fine at lower resolutions - **4x performance gain!**
:::

## Format Selection

```cpp
// Choose appropriate format for your needs

// LDR color (most common)
GL_RGB8 or GL_RGBA8

// HDR rendering
GL_RGB16F (sufficient for most cases)
GL_RGB32F (highest quality, more memory)

// Normals
GL_RGB16F (signed values, good precision)

// Depth only
GL_DEPTH_COMPONENT24 or GL_DEPTH_COMPONENT32F
```

---

# Common Issues

## Troubleshooting

::: {.panel-tabset}
### Black Screen
```cpp
// Check framebuffer status
if (glCheckFramebufferStatus(GL_FRAMEBUFFER) != GL_FRAMEBUFFER_COMPLETE) {
    std::cerr << "Framebuffer not complete!\n";
}

// Verify texture attachment
glFramebufferTexture2D(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, GL_COLOR_ATTACHMENT0, ...);

// Check viewport
glViewport(0, 0, width, height);
```

### Artifacts
```cpp
// Ensure proper filtering
glTexParameteri(GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_MIN_FILTER, GL_LINEAR);
glTexParameteri(GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_MAG_FILTER, GL_LINEAR);

// Check texture wrapping
glTexParameteri(GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_WRAP_S, GL_CLAMP_TO_EDGE);
glTexParameteri(GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_WRAP_T, GL_CLAMP_TO_EDGE);
```

### Performance
```cpp
// Reduce resolution for expensive effects
// Use simpler kernels
// Profile with RenderDoc or similar
// Consider GPU capabilities
```
:::

---

# Practical Applications

## Real-World Uses

::: {.incremental}
- **Games**: Bloom, motion blur, depth of field, lens effects
- **Photo editing**: Filters, effects, adjustments
- **Video processing**: Real-time effects, color grading
- **AR/VR**: Lens distortion correction, foveated rendering
- **Scientific visualization**: False color, enhancement
- **Medical imaging**: Enhancement, filtering
:::

## Effect Combinations

```cpp
// Typical game rendering pipeline
1. Render scene to HDR framebuffer
2. Extract bright areas for bloom
3. Blur bright areas (multiple passes)
4. Tone map HDR to LDR
5. Apply bloom
6. Apply color grading
7. Apply vignette
8. Apply film grain/chromatic aberration
9. Final output to screen
```

---

# What We've Learned

## Key Concepts

::: {.incremental}
- **Framebuffers**: Render to texture
- **Post-processing**: Screen-space effects
- **Kernel effects**: Convolution filters
- **Gaussian blur**: High-quality blur
- **Bloom**: Glow effect
- **HDR and tone mapping**: Wide range colors
- **MRT**: Multiple outputs
- **Performance**: Optimization strategies
:::

## Next Steps

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Practice:**

- Implement basic effects
- Combine multiple effects
- Create effect presets
- Experiment with parameters
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Advanced:**

- Deferred rendering
- SSAO (Screen-Space Ambient Occlusion)
- SSR (Screen-Space Reflections)
- Temporal anti-aliasing
- Compute shader post-processing
:::
:::

## Resources

**Learning:**

- [Learn OpenGL - Framebuffers](https://learnopengl.com/Advanced-OpenGL/Framebuffers)
- GPU Gems (post-processing chapters)
- ShaderToy for inspiration
- Real-time rendering techniques papers

**Tools:**

- RenderDoc - Frame analysis
- NVIDIA Nsight - Profiling
- ShaderToy - Effect prototyping

---

# Questions?

::: {.r-fit-text}
Thank you!
:::

::: {.fragment}
Post-processing effects transform raw renders into polished, professional visuals!
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Keep experimenting** with different effects and combinations! 🎨
:::
